name: Scotch Black Bun
recipeInstructions:
1. The butter, cut into small pieces.
2. In a medium-sized mixing bowl, combine the butter, 3 3/4 cups flour, and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Mix with fingers until uniform granules form.
3. Sprinkle 8 tablespoons of ice water over the granules and continue mixing until a soft ball forms. Add more ice water if needed.
4. Press dough into a thick cake, wrap in plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
5. Prepare filling by sifting 3 1/2 cups flour, sugar, baking powder, spices (cinnamon, mace, allspice), and salt into a large bowl. Add black pepper, raisins (seedless and white or golden), ground almonds, and coarsely chopped almonds.
6. Mix thoroughly until all ingredients are coated well with dry ingredients.
7. Add 3 eggs, milk, and brandy; mix well.
8. Preheat oven to 375°F.
9. Roll two-thirds of the chilled pastry to a thickness of 1/4-inch on a lightly floured surface. Place in a 9 x 3 1/2-inch springform pan.
10. Cut along the outside of the pan to form a disk, then line the bottom of the pan with it. Use remaining pastry pieces to line the sides of the pan, slightly overlapping and extending about 1/2 inch above the top.
11. Spoon filling into the pan, packing firmly. Press excess pastry over the filling.
12. Brush pastry with beaten egg. Roll remaining pastry into a circle larger than the form, unroll on top, and press against the filling.
13. Trim excess pastry. Brush entire top with beaten egg.
14. Cut a 1-inch round vent in the center of the bun. Use scraps to make leaf shapes, trim leaves with beaten egg, place on top, and brush again with beaten egg.
15. Bake on a rack in the middle of the oven for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or until golden brown. If browning is uneven, cover decorations with aluminum foil.
